data.title: Boliden (OM:BOL) Margin Decline Challenges Bullish Growth Narratives In Q1 2026
data.description:
Boliden (OM:BOL) opened 2026 with Q1 revenue of SEK 27.8b and basic EPS of SEK 13.45, alongside net income of SEK 3.8b, putting fresh numbers behind its latest production and pricing mix. The company has seen revenue move from SEK 25.8b in Q4 2024 to SEK 28.1b in Q4 2025 and then SEK 27.8b in Q1 2026, while basic EPS shifted from SEK 10.95 to SEK 15.31 and then SEK 13.45 over the same reported periods. This gives investors a clearer view of how earnings are tracking into the new year as net profit margins sit in the low double digits.

See our full analysis for Boliden.

Next is a look at how these results line up with the prevailing risk and reward narratives around Boliden, and where the numbers either back those stories or start to push against them.

See what the community is saying about Boliden

Advertisement

Margin Picture Through The Cycle

  • On a trailing basis, Boliden generated SEK 11.0b in net income on SEK 100.2b of revenue, which works out to an 11% net profit margin compared with 12.1% a year earlier.
  • Consensus narrative expects margins to improve over time. However, the recent slip from 12.1% to 11% shows that, while projects like Odda and the recycling expansions aim to reduce costs and support earnings resilience, the latest twelve month margin outcome is still below that earlier level.
    • Analysts link future margin support to automation and digitalization, but current trailing EPS of SEK 38.84 is only modestly ahead of the prior trailing figure of SEK 36.65.
    • The same consensus view sees ramp ups at Tara, Kristineberg, and Rönnskär as helpful for long term efficiency, while the data still shows five year earnings declining at about 1.1% per year.

Q1 EPS Swings And Bullish Growth Hopes

  • Quarterly EPS has been quite choppy, moving from SEK 2.02 in Q2 2025 to SEK 8.07 in Q3 2025, SEK 15.31 in Q4 2025, and then SEK 13.45 in Q1 2026, while revenue ranged between SEK 21.1b and SEK 28.1b over the same stretch.
  • Bullish investors argue that project execution and low carbon positioning can support much stronger earnings growth, and the recent move in trailing EPS to SEK 38.84 compared with SEK 33.39 a quarter earlier gives them some numerical backing, even though the past five years still show an average 1.1% earnings decline.
    • Bullish narratives point to production and cost benefits from assets like Tara, Kristineberg, Odda and Rönnskär, and the TTM net income of SEK 11.0b is higher than the SEK 9.4b figure cited as a current baseline in those growth scenarios.
    • Supporters also highlight that forecasts call for earnings growth of about 13.3% per year, which sits in contrast to the negative most recent one year earnings growth flagged in the data.
Have a closer look at how the optimistic case lines up with these earnings swings and margin trends by checking out the 🐂 Boliden Bull Case

Valuation Gap Versus Profit Trends

  • Boliden trades on a trailing P/E of 12.8x with a current share price of SEK 498.10, while the DCF fair value is given as SEK 2,316.95 and the analyst price target as SEK 547.18, at the same time that trailing net margin sits at 11% and has eased from 12.1%.
  • Bears focus on the softer margin and modest five year earnings decline, and the valuation gap to the DCF fair value of SEK 2,316.95 alongside the smaller gap to the SEK 547.18 analyst target gives them room to argue that some of the discount may reflect concerns about earnings volatility and an unstable dividend record rather than pure mispricing.
    • Critics point out that while revenue over the last twelve months totals SEK 100.2b, past five year earnings have still declined by about 1.1% per year despite this scale.
    • The bearish narrative also highlights that the latest twelve month margin of 11% is lower than the prior 12.1%, which is consistent with worries about pressure from ore grades, energy costs, and regulation.
If you want to see how skeptics connect these valuation gaps to operational risks and margins, take a look at the 🐻 Boliden Bear Case

data.title: Brown And Brown (BRO) Margin Compression Challenges Bullish Earnings Growth Narrative In Q1 2026
data.description:
Brown & Brown (BRO) kicked off Q1 2026 with total revenue of US$1.9 billion and basic EPS of US$1.27, alongside trailing twelve month figures of US$6.3 billion in revenue and US$3.53 in EPS that frame the latest quarter in a bigger context. Over recent periods, revenue has moved from US$1.2 billion in Q4 2024 to US$1.6 billion in Q4 2025 and US$1.9 billion in Q1 2026. Quarterly basic EPS has shifted from US$0.73 to US$0.78 and now US$1.27, setting up a story where investors will be weighing earnings power against how margins are holding up.

See our full analysis for Brown & Brown.

With the headline numbers on the table, the next step is to see how this earnings profile lines up with the widely followed growth, risk, and valuation narratives around Brown & Brown.

See what the community is saying about Brown & Brown

Advertisement

Net income and margins cooling from prior year

  • On a trailing basis, Brown & Brown earned US$1.1b of net income on US$6.3b of revenue, which works out to an 18.2% net profit margin compared with 21% in the prior year.
  • Analysts' consensus view points to earnings stability supported by cost discipline and debt repayment, yet the 18.2% trailing margin versus 21% a year ago and quarterly net income of US$421 million versus US$327 million in Q1 2025 shows that margin compression is sitting alongside higher profit dollars.
    • Consensus commentary highlights an improved EBITDAC margin by 110 basis points and reduced interest expense, while the reported net margin has moved lower year on year, so efficiency gains are being partially offset elsewhere in the income statement.
    • The same consensus view talks about revenue opportunities in employee benefits and CAT property, but the current 18.2% net margin suggests that any benefit from those areas is not fully flowing through to the bottom line yet.

Multi year earnings growth vs slower outlook

  • Over the past five years, earnings grew at 15.9% per year, while trailing 12 month earnings growth sits at 11.6% and forecasts point to around 8.2% earnings growth and 8.3% revenue growth per year.
  • Analysts' consensus narrative leans positive on future expansion through acquisitions and strong demand in employee benefits, but the moderation from 15.9% historical earnings growth to 11.6% recently and about 8.2% expected highlights that the growth story is becoming more gradual than the past run rate.
    • The company completed 13 acquisitions with projected annual revenue of US$36 million, which is small compared with US$6.3b of trailing revenue, so these deals may fine tune growth more than transform it.
    • Consensus expects ongoing demand for benefits consulting and broader revenue expansion, yet the forecast 8.3% revenue growth remains below broader US market forecasts cited, so investors are being asked to pay attention to quality of growth rather than sheer speed.
Brown & Brown's earnings path tells only part of the story, and many investors are watching how the community weighs growth, margins, and valuation in one place, especially after this set of numbers. See what the community is saying about Brown & Brown.

P/E premium and DCF gap to current price

  • At a share price of US$63.14, the trailing P/E of 18.8x sits above the US Insurance industry average of 11.6x but below the peer group average of 35.7x, while a DCF fair value of US$138.64 and an analyst target of US$78.33 both stand above the current price.
  • Consensus narrative notes that effective cost management and cash generation help support the case for this valuation, yet the premium P/E, 18.2% net margin that is lower than last year, and balance sheet flags around debt coverage and shareholder dilution all mean the market is weighing mixed signals rather than a one way quality story.
    • On the risk side, debt is described as not well covered by operating cash flow and shareholders were diluted over the past year, so the balance sheet is an active part of the discussion, not an afterthought.
    • On the reward side, the model DCF fair value of US$138.64 and analyst target of US$78.33 both sit above US$63.14, which contrasts with the softer margin profile and slower growth forecasts and helps explain why opinions on the current P/E premium are likely divided.

Next Steps

data.description:

AVADA Group Insider Transactions Over The Last Year

In the last twelve months, the biggest single sale by an insider was when the Founder & Executive Director, Daniel Crowley, sold AU$944k worth of shares at a price of AU$0.20 per share. While insider selling is a negative, to us, it is more negative if the shares are sold at a lower price. The silver lining is that this sell-down took place above the latest price (AU$0.09). So it is hard to draw any strong conclusion from it. Daniel Crowley was the only individual insider to sell over the last year. Notably Daniel Crowley was also the biggest buyer, having purchased AU$302k worth of shares.

Over the last year, we can see that insiders have bought 3.16m shares worth AU$302k. But insiders sold 14.36m shares worth AU$944k. data.title: Here's Why We're Watching Modalis Therapeutics' (TSE:4883) Cash Burn Situation
data.description:
There's no doubt that money can be made by owning shares of unprofitable businesses. For example, although software-as-a-service business Salesforce.com lost money for years while it grew recurring revenue, if you held shares since 2005, you'd have done very well indeed. But the harsh reality is that very many loss making companies burn through all their cash and go bankrupt.

Given this risk, we thought we'd take a look at whether Modalis Therapeutics (TSE:4883) shareholders should be worried about its cash burn. For the purpose of this article, we'll define cash burn as the amount of cash the company is spending each year to fund its growth (also called its negative free cash flow). Let's start with an examination of the business' cash, relative to its cash burn.

Trump has pledged to "unleash" American oil and gas and these 15 US stocks have developments that are poised to benefit.

Advertisement

How Long Is Modalis Therapeutics' Cash Runway?

You can calculate a company's cash runway by dividing the amount of cash it has by the rate at which it is spending that cash. As at December 2025, Modalis Therapeutics had cash of JP¥2.8b and no debt. Looking at the last year, the company burnt through JP¥2.1b. Therefore, from December 2025 it had roughly 16 months of cash runway. While that cash runway isn't too concerning, sensible holders would be peering into the distance, and considering what happens if the company runs out of cash. You can see how its cash balance has changed over time in the image below.

View our latest analysis for Modalis Therapeutics

How Is Modalis Therapeutics' Cash Burn Changing Over Time?

Modalis Therapeutics didn't record any revenue over the last year, indicating that it's an early stage company still developing its business. Nonetheless, we can still examine its cash burn trajectory as part of our assessment of its cash burn situation. With the cash burn rate up 48% in the last year, it seems that the company is ratcheting up investment in the business over time. However, the company's true cash runway will therefore be shorter than suggested above, if spending continues to increase. Admittedly, we're a bit cautious of Modalis Therapeutics due to its lack of significant operating revenues. So we'd generally prefer stocks from this list of stocks that have analysts forecasting growth.

How Hard Would It Be For Modalis Therapeutics To Raise More Cash For Growth?

While Modalis Therapeutics does have a solid cash runway, its cash burn trajectory may have some shareholders thinking ahead to when the company may need to raise more cash. Issuing new shares, or taking on debt, are the most common ways for a listed company to raise more money for its business. Many companies end up issuing new shares to fund future growth. By comparing a company's annual cash burn to its total market capitalisation, we can estimate roughly how many shares it would have to issue in order to run the company for another year (at the same burn rate).

Modalis Therapeutics has a market capitalisation of JP¥5.9b and burnt through JP¥2.1b last year, which is 36% of the company's market value. That's fairly notable cash burn, so if the company had to sell shares to cover the cost of another year's operations, shareholders would suffer some costly dilution.

So, Should We Worry About Modalis Therapeutics' Cash Burn?

On this analysis of Modalis Therapeutics' cash burn, we think its cash runway was reassuring, while its cash burn relative to its market cap has us a bit worried. Summing up, we think the Modalis Therapeutics' cash burn is a risk, based on the factors we mentioned in this article.     • The idea of improving operational leverage, set against net losses that have widened on the latest 12 month view compared with the prior trailing period.

Quarterly swing from small profit to larger loss

  • Q1 2026 net loss was US$19.6 million on revenue of US$61.5 million, compared with Q3 2025 net income of US$0.7 million on US$60.1 million of revenue and Q4 2025 net loss of US$13.2 million on US$59.9 million of revenue.
  • Bullish views highlight potential for better operating leverage as sales build, yet the recent pattern of quarterly results raises questions about how smooth that path might be, because:
    • Profitability flipped from a small profit in Q2 and Q3 2025, with EPS of about US$0.01 per share in each of those quarters, to losses again in Q4 2025 and Q1 2026, including a Q1 2026 loss of US$0.38 per share.
    • The move from roughly breakeven to a larger quarterly loss suggests the margin profile is still sensitive even at revenue levels around US$60 million per quarter.
Curious how this earnings swing fits into the bullish case that highlights margin improvement and long term growth potential, and see how supporters frame the upside in spite of the latest loss print: 🐂 Axogen Bull Case.

  • Bearish arguments focus on valuation and dilution, and the current data provide clear support for that caution, because:
    • Shareholders have already seen their ownership diluted over the past year, which matters more when the company is still loss making on both the latest quarter and the trailing 12 month figures.
    • The gap between the current price and the DCF fair value, alongside higher P/S multiples than peers and the broader industry, underpins concerns that a lot of the projected growth is already reflected in the valuation.

data.title: METAWATER Co., Ltd. (TSE:9551) Analysts Are Pretty Bullish On The Stock After Recent Results
data.description:
Last week, you might have seen that METAWATER Co., Ltd. (TSE:9551) released its full-year result to the market. The early response was not positive, with shares down 7.5% to JP¥3,415 in the past week. Results overall were respectable, with statutory earnings of JP¥209 per share roughly in line with what the analysts had forecast. Revenues of JP¥210b came in 3.4% ahead of analyst predictions. This is an important time for investors, as they can track a company's performance in its report, look at what experts are forecasting for next year, and see if there has been any change to expectations for the business. So we gathered the latest post-earnings forecasts to see what estimates suggest is in store for next year.

We've found 21 US stocks that are forecast to pay a dividend yield of over 6% next year. See the full list for free.

Following the latest results, METAWATER's five analysts are now forecasting revenues of JP¥220.7b in 2027. This would be a modest 5.2% improvement in revenue compared to the last 12 months. Per-share earnings are expected to increase 5.0% to JP¥220. Yet prior to the latest earnings, the analysts had been anticipated revenues of JP¥217.4b and earnings per share (EPS) of JP¥209 in 2027. So the consensus seems to have become somewhat more optimistic on METAWATER's earnings potential following these results.

Check out our latest analysis for METAWATER

The consensus price target rose 17% to JP¥3,100, suggesting that higher earnings estimates flow through to the stock's valuation as well. It could also be instructive to look at the range of analyst estimates, to evaluate how different the outlier opinions are from the mean. Currently, the most bullish analyst values METAWATER at JP¥3,800 per share, while the most bearish prices it at JP¥2,000. Analysts definitely have varying views on the business, but the spread of estimates is not wide enough in our view to suggest that extreme outcomes could await METAWATER shareholders.

Looking at the bigger picture now, one of the ways we can make sense of these forecasts is to see how they measure up against both past performance and industry growth estimates. We would highlight that METAWATER's revenue growth is expected to slow, with the forecast 5.2% annualised growth rate until the end of 2027 being well below the historical 9.0% p.a. growth over the last five years. Compare this to the 247 other companies in this industry with analyst coverage, which are forecast to grow their revenue at 5.7% per year. Factoring in the forecast slowdown in growth, it looks like METAWATER is forecast to grow at about the same rate as the wider industry.

Advertisement

The Bottom Line

The biggest takeaway for us is the consensus earnings per share upgrade, which suggests a clear improvement in sentiment around METAWATER's earnings potential next year. They also reconfirmed their revenue estimates, with the company predicted to grow at about the same rate as the wider industry. We note an upgrade to the price target, suggesting that the analysts believes the intrinsic value of the business is likely to improve over time.

With that said, the long-term trajectory of the company's earnings is a lot more important than next year. At Simply Wall St, we have a full range of analyst estimates for METAWATER going out to 2029, and you can see them free on our platform here..

We don't want to rain on the parade too much, but we did also find 1 warning sign for METAWATER that you need to be mindful of.

data.title: Is It Too Late To Consider AL Sydbank (CPSE:ALSYDB) After Its 37% One-Year Surge?
data.description:
• If you are wondering whether AL Sydbank at around kr.544.50 is still offering value or if most of the opportunity is already priced in, you are in the right place.
  • The share price has moved around recently, with a 3% decline over the last 7 days, an 8.4% gain over the last 30 days, a 4.6% decline year to date, and a 37.2% return over the past year, capped by very large gains over 3 and 5 years.
  • These swings have been accompanied by ongoing interest in the Danish banking sector and investor focus on how balance sheet strength, funding costs, and capital returns shape bank valuations. For AL Sydbank, this context matters because it can influence how the market treats traditional valuation metrics such as P/E, P/B, and dividend yield.
  • AL Sydbank currently has a value score of 3 out of 6. The key question is what different valuation approaches say about that score today, and whether there is an even better way to think about value that will be covered at the end of this article.

Find out why AL Sydbank's 37.2% return over the last year is lagging behind its peers.

Advertisement

Approach 1: AL Sydbank Excess Returns Analysis

The Excess Returns model looks at how much value a bank can create over and above the return that shareholders require. It starts with the equity invested in the business and compares the returns generated on that equity to the estimated cost of equity.

For AL Sydbank, the model uses a Book Value of DKK416.84 per share and an Average Return on Equity of 12.81%. This leads to a Stable EPS estimate of DKK59.88 per share, based on weighted future Return on Equity estimates from 4 analysts. The Cost of Equity is set at DKK28.83 per share, which implies an Excess Return of DKK31.05 per share. The Stable Book Value used in the model is DKK467.39 per share, also drawn from weighted future Book Value estimates from 4 analysts.

Combining these inputs, the Excess Returns valuation points to an intrinsic value of roughly DKK1,187.97 per share. With a current share price of around DKK544.50, this corresponds to the stock being 54.2% undervalued according to this model.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Our Excess Returns analysis suggests AL Sydbank is undervalued by 54.2%. Track this in your watchlist or portfolio, or discover 237 more high quality undervalued stocks.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for AL Sydbank.

Approach 2: AL Sydbank Price vs Earnings

For a profitable bank like AL Sydbank, the P/E ratio is a useful way to think about value because it links what you pay directly to the earnings the business is already generating. In general, higher expected growth and lower perceived risk can justify a higher P/E, while lower growth expectations or higher risk usually align with a lower, more conservative P/E.

AL Sydbank currently trades on a P/E of 25.37x. This is above the Danish Banks industry average P/E of 11.01x and also above the peer average of 10.10x. Simply Wall St’s Fair Ratio for AL Sydbank is 17.74x. This Fair Ratio is a proprietary estimate of what the P/E might be based on factors such as earnings growth, profit margins, the bank’s industry, market capitalization and specific risk profile, rather than simple comparisons with peers that may have very different fundamentals.

Comparing the actual P/E of 25.37x with the Fair Ratio of 17.74x suggests the shares are trading above what the company’s characteristics would typically support.

Result: OVERVALUED

Upgrade Your Decision Making: Choose your AL Sydbank Narrative

Earlier it was mentioned that there is an even better way to understand valuation, so this is where Narratives come in. This is a simple tool that lets you set out your story about AL Sydbank, including your assumptions for future revenue, earnings, margins and fair value, then tie that story directly to a financial forecast and a fair value that you can compare to the current share price.

On Simply Wall St, within the Community page used by millions of investors, Narratives make this process accessible by guiding you to link your view of the business to numbers. This can help you decide whether the gap between your Fair Value and the current price suggests you might wait, build a position or consider taking profits, and your Narrative is then automatically updated when new information such as news or earnings is released.

For AL Sydbank, one investor might build a Narrative that supports a relatively high fair value based on their expectations for returns on equity and capital returns. Another might set a much lower fair value if they are more cautious about future profitability and the bank’s ability to sustain current metrics, showing how the same data can lead to very different, but clearly articulated, views.

data.title: Franklin Electric (FELE) One Off Loss And 6.9% Margin Test Bullish Growth Narrative
data.description:
Franklin Electric (FELE) has opened 2026 with Q1 revenue of US$500.4 million and basic EPS of US$0.77, alongside trailing twelve month revenue of about US$2.2 billion and EPS of US$3.36 that frame the latest quarter in a broader context. Over the past year, quarterly revenue has ranged from US$455.2 million to US$587.4 million, while basic EPS has moved between US$0.37 and US$1.32, setting a clear reference point for how this new print fits into the recent run rate. With a trailing net margin of 6.9% versus 8.8% a year earlier, investors are likely to focus on how efficiently Franklin Electric is turning that revenue base into profits in the periods ahead.

See our full analysis for Franklin Electric.

With the headline numbers on the table, the next step is to see how they line up against the widely followed narratives around Franklin Electric's growth prospects, risk profile, and earnings quality.

See what the community is saying about Franklin Electric

Advertisement

Margins Shaped By One Off Loss

  • Over the last 12 months, Franklin Electric earned US$149.9 million of net income on US$2.2b of revenue, giving a 6.9% net margin compared with 8.8% a year earlier, and this period includes a US$61.8 million one off loss that weighs on those figures.
  • Consensus narrative talks about cost optimization and higher margin products lifting profitability, which sits in tension with the recent 6.9% margin and the one off loss:
    • On the supportive side, the focus on energy efficient pumping technologies and digital monitoring is framed as a way to build higher margin, more recurring revenue over time.
    • On the balancing side, acquisitions and product mix shifts, including dewatering equipment, are cited as sources of margin pressure that help explain why the current margin is below the prior 8.8% level.

EPS Trend And Earnings Quality Check

  • Across the last five quarters, basic EPS has moved between US$0.37 and US$1.32, landing at US$0.77 in Q1 2026, while trailing 12 month EPS sits at US$3.36, all of which is being assessed against forecasts that point to roughly 22% annual EPS growth.
  • Analysts' consensus view highlights strong expected earnings growth, yet the recent EPS pattern and the one off loss introduce a quality check on that optimism:
    • On the supportive side for a bullish view, trailing EPS of US$3.36 and net income of US$149.9 million are compared with higher future earnings expectations in the narrative, which sees room for growth from this base.
    • On the cautious side, the large US$61.8 million one off loss and the wide quarterly EPS range remind you that part of the trailing record is affected by non recurring items, so the backward looking growth rate is not a clean read on the business trend.

Bulls and skeptics are looking at the same EPS path and one off loss yet reaching very different conclusions about what is sustainable over the next few years, so it helps to see how the detailed bullish thesis stacks up against the raw numbers in this report.🐂 Franklin Electric Bull Case

Mixed Signals From Valuation Benchmarks

  • Using the current share price of US$103.05 and trailing EPS of US$3.36, the data implies a P/E of about 30.4x, compared with a Machinery industry average of 28.1x, a peer average of 64.3x, and a DCF fair value of roughly US$100.95.
  • Bears focus on valuation stretch and margin risk, and the numbers give them both support and pushback:
    • Supporting the bearish angle, the P/E sits slightly above the Machinery industry and the share price is modestly above the DCF fair value, while the 6.9% trailing margin is lower than the prior 8.8% level.
    • Challenging the more cautious view, the same P/E is lower than the 64.3x peer average in the data, and analysts still model strong EPS growth, so valuation does not sit at an extreme relative to that narrower group.

If you are weighing whether the recent P/E and margin profile really justify a cautious stance, it is worth seeing how bearish arguments line up against the detailed risk factors and segment trends in the full narrative.🐻 Franklin Electric Bear Case

Next Steps

Struck by how balanced the picture is between opportunity and concern, use the figures here as a starting point and pressure test the bullish and bearish narratives against your own expectations. Then round out that view by checking the 1 key reward and 1 important warning sign.

See What Else Is Out There

Franklin Electric's recent one off loss, lower 6.9% net margin versus 8.8%, wide EPS range, and P/E above the industry average all point to valuation and earnings quality pressure.

If those trade offs make you hesitant to pay up for this story, it is worth comparing with companies screened as 53 high quality undervalued stocks right now.
